name: Bella's Pickleball
description: Come play pickleball at Bella's Pickleball in Kennewick, WA! There are 8 indoor courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. You can’t reserve the courts. Amenities include a pro shop or equipment store, food, restrooms, water, lights, trainers or lessons, youth programming, and locker rooms.

name: Benton City Pickleball Courts
description: Benton City Pickleball Courts is a place to play pickleball in Benton City, WA. There are 6 outdoor concrete courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. Amenities include lights and wheelchair accessibility. The courts are free.

name: Canyon Lakes
description: Come play pickleball at Canyon Lakes in Kennewick, WA! There is 1 outdoor court. This is a dedicated court with a permanent net and lines. The court is free.

name: Claybell Park
description: Claybell Park is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Richland, WA. There are 6 outdoor hard courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. The facility includes amenities like restrooms and water. The courts are free.

name: Columbia Basin Raquet Club
description: Columbia Basin Raquet Club is a place to play pickleball in Richland, WA. There are 12 concrete courts. 6 are indoors and 6 are outdoors. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. A membership is required to play. You can reserve the courts. The facility includes amenities like food, locker rooms, restrooms, lights, water, youth programming, and trainers or lessons.

name: Heights Elementary
description: Come play pickleball at Heights Elementary in Prosser, WA! There are 3 indoor courts. The lines are permanent, but you'll need to bring your own net. The courts are private.

name: Horn Rapids
description: Restrooms are seasonal. Opened in spring once freezing temps are over and closed mid-October.

name: Horse Heaven School Park
description: Horse Heaven School Park is a place to play pickleball in Kennewick, WA. There are 2 outdoor hard courts. The lines are overlaid on a tennis court, so the net is a little taller than a normal pickleball net. Amenities include lights. The courts are free.

name: Lawrence Scott Park - Club 509
description: Lawrence Scott Park - Club 509 is a place to play pickleball in Kennewick, WA. There are 15 outdoor hard courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. Players have access to restrooms, water, lights, and wheelchair accessibility. Reservations are available. The courts are free.

name: Lawrence Scott Park Pickleball Complex
description: An excellent facility supported also by 509 Pickleball Club. Major downside is that the players often do not abide by rack up or challenge rules. They like to bring four or a group and “own” the court. If you’re good enough to beat or challenge then they’ll never know. It’s better and more hospitable in other cities where a local parks municipality governs and manages play so that people are not excluded.

name: OLD Prosser High School
description: OLD Prosser High School is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Prosser, WA. There are 6 outdoor concrete courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. The courts are free.

name: Southridge Sports Complex
description: Southridge Sports Complex is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Kennewick, WA. There are 11 hard courts. 8 are outdoors and 3 are indoors. The lines are permanent, but you'll need to bring your own net. The courts can be reserved. A one-time fee is required to play. Players have access to lights.

name: Sunset Park
description: Sunset Park is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Kennewick, WA. There are 2 outdoor concrete courts. The lines are overlaid on a tennis court, so the net is a little taller than a normal pickleball net. The courts are free. You can’t reserve the courts. Players have access to restrooms and water.

name: The Pacific Clinic
description: The Pacific Clinic is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Kennewick, WA. There are 6 indoor concrete courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. You can reserve the courts. Players have access to food, restrooms, water, and wheelchair accessibility. A membership is required to play.
